briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: To define the abstract base class for all metadata extractors, establishing the plugin contract (can_extract + extract) that every format-specific extractor must implement.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Scope\/ \: Abstract extractor interface, file-name resolution, format-matching logic. \par \b Input\/ \: Constructor: config dict, recognized_extensions set, recognized_mimes set. \par \b Output\/ \: Abstract contract consumed by MetadataExtractorComposition and concrete extractors. \par \b Links\/ \: [USES_API(6): os.path, dedoc.utils.get_mime_extension] \par \b Invariants\/ \:

listitem.para: can_extract checks BOTH extension AND mime before returning bool.
_get_names always returns a 4-tuple (dir, name, converted_name, original_name). \par \b Rationale\/ \:

detaileddescription.para: Q: Why use Abstract Base Class with abstractmethod extract? A: Enforces Liskov substitution — every extractor in the composition pipeline exposes the same extract() signature, regardless of format. \par \b Changes\/ \:
LAST_CHANGE: [v1.0.0 — Semantic template markup and LDD logging added.] \par \b Module Map\/ \:
CLASS 10[Abstract base for all metadata extractors] => AbstractMetadataExtractor \par \b Use Cases\/ \:
